agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Multi-step orchestration with zero error handling guarantees orphaned resources and inconsistent state on any partial failure"
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Partial failure leaves orphaned courier reservation with no compensation",
      "risk": "If any step after `courierService.reserve()` throws, the courier remains reserved but the order is never assigned and no dispatch occurs. The courier is effectively locked out of the system — they receive no orders and no one releases them. Under load, this drains the courier pool until no couriers are available.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "assignAndDispatch, between steps 1-4",
      "reproduction": "1. Call assignAndDispatch('order-1'). 2. Have orderService.updateStatus throw (e.g., DB timeout). 3. Courier is now reserved but order status is unchanged. 4. No rollback of the reservation occurs. 5. Repeat under load — courier pool drains to zero.",
      "fix": "Wrap steps 2-4 in a try/catch. On failure, call `courierService.release(courierId)` as compensation. Use a finally or catch block pattern:\n\n```typescript\nasync function assignAndDispatch(orderId: string): Promise<void> {\n  const courierId = await courierService.reserve(orderId);\n  try {\n    await orderService.updateStatus(orderId, 'ASSIGNED', courierId);\n    await notificationService.sendDispatchAlert(courierId, orderId);\n    await notificationService.sendCustomerAlert(orderId, courierId);\n  } catch (err) {\n    await courierService.release(courierId).catch(() => {});\n    throw err;\n  }\n}\n```",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Courier availability, order assignment reliability"
    },
    {
      "title": "Non-idempotent operation — retries create duplicate reservations",
      "risk": "When the caller retries after a transient failure (e.g., step 3 notification timeout), `courierService.reserve()` is called again for the same orderId. This reserves a second courier while the first remains orphaned. Two couriers now believe they are assigned to the same order.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "assignAndDispatch, step 1 — courierService.reserve(orderId)",
      "reproduction": "1. Call assignAndDispatch('order-1'). 2. Steps 1-2 succeed. Step 3 (sendDispatchAlert) times out. 3. Caller retries assignAndDispatch('order-1'). 4. A new courier is reserved for the same order. 5. Two couriers are reserved; first is orphaned, second gets dispatched.",
      "fix": "Add an idempotency guard at the top of the function: check if the order already has a courier assigned before reserving a new one. Alternatively, make `courierService.reserve(orderId)` itself idempotent — return the existing reservation if one exists for that orderId.\n\n```typescript\nconst existing = await orderService.getAssignedCourier(orderId);\nif (existing) return; // already assigned, skip\n```",
      "time_to_fix": "1 hour",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Exactly-once courier assignment, courier pool integrity"
    },
    {
      "title": "Notification failure blocks the entire dispatch — customer/courier left in limbo",
      "risk": "Steps 3 and 4 are notification side-effects, yet they are treated as hard requirements. If sendDispatchAlert throws (push service down, rate limited), the function throws — but the order IS already marked ASSIGNED in step 2. The order is assigned with no dispatch sent: the courier never knows, the customer never knows, and no retry mechanism exists for the notifications alone.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "assignAndDispatch, steps 3-4",
      "reproduction": "1. Steps 1-2 succeed (courier reserved, order marked ASSIGNED). 2. notificationService.sendDispatchAlert throws (push service 503). 3. Function throws. 4. Order is ASSIGNED but courier was never notified. 5. If compensation from finding #1 is added, the courier gets released — but the order status is still ASSIGNED to a now-released courier.",
      "fix": "Separate the transaction boundary from side-effects. Steps 1-2 are the critical transaction (reserve + update). Steps 3-4 should be fire-and-forget or queued for retry independently. At minimum, wrap notifications in individual try/catch blocks and log failures for async retry:\n\n```typescript\n// Core transaction\nconst courierId = await courierService.reserve(orderId);\nawait orderService.updateStatus(orderId, 'ASSIGNED', courierId);\n\n// Side-effects — best-effort with retry queue\nawait Promise.allSettled([\n  notificationService.sendDispatchAlert(courierId, orderId),\n  notificationService.sendCustomerAlert(orderId, courierId),\n]).then(results => {\n  results.filter(r => r.status === 'rejected')\n    .forEach(r => retryQueue.enqueue(/* ... */));\n});\n```",
      "time_to_fix": "1 hour",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Courier dispatch reliability, customer communication SLA"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No timeout or deadline on the overall operation",
      "description": "Four sequential awaits with no timeout. If any service hangs (e.g., courierService.reserve blocks for 30s waiting for a free courier), the caller has no way to bail out. Under backpressure this queues up unboundedly.",
      "location": "assignAndDispatch — all four await calls"
    },
    {
      "title": "Compensation rollback itself can fail",
      "description": "Even after adding the catch-and-release pattern from finding #1, the `courierService.release()` call can itself fail (network partition, service down). A dead-letter or periodic reconciliation job is needed to sweep orphaned reservations.",
      "location": "assignAndDispatch — error handling path"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Add structured logging at each step boundary so partial failures can be diagnosed from logs alone",
    "Consider a saga/workflow pattern (e.g., Temporal, or a state machine) if this flow grows more steps — sequential try/catch compensation does not scale past 4-5 steps",
    "Emit domain events (OrderAssigned, CourierDispatched) instead of inline notification calls to decouple the transaction from delivery"
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "No error handling means any transient failure (DB blip, push service 503) orphans couriers and corrupts order state. Under real traffic, courier pool will drain within minutes of an outage in any downstream service.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — no deployment artifact to roll back. The logic itself has no rollback/compensation mechanism.",
    "backward_compatibility": "N/A — standalone function review",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging or metrics. Orphaned couriers and stuck orders will be invisible until customers complain.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "N/A — must fix before shipping. No safe rollback exists for data inconsistency caused by partial failures."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 95,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Orphaned reservation on partial failure — deterministic with any step 2/3/4 error",
      "Duplicate reservation on retry — no idempotency guard exists",
      "Notification failure corrupts state — observable from code structure alone"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether courierService.reserve is already idempotent internally — cannot verify without service implementation"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "courierService.reserve() is not idempotent (no dedup on orderId)",
      "No external retry/saga orchestrator wraps this function",
      "No dead-letter queue or reconciliation job exists to sweep orphaned reservations"
    ]
  }
}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this code carefully and produce my review.

Classification: `none` — the fixes are standard error handling patterns (compensation/rollback, try-catch, idempotency). No architectural concerns.

Key hypotheses:
1. No compensation/rollback on partial failure — if step 2 fails, courier is reserved but order isn't updated (orphaned reservation)
2. No idempotency — retrying the function could double-reserve
3. Silent failure modes — caller gets a rejected promise but no cleanup happens

Let me write the output directly.
